content.full_text:
January 28, 1977

Thomas F. Winfield III, Esq.
Ebben and Brown
615 South Flower Street, Suite 1201
Los Angeles, CA 90013

Dear Mr. Winfield:

Please excuse my delay in responding to your letter of January 6, 1977. It apparently was misplaced when it arrived and I just received it.

I think your letter accurately reflects the substance of our telephone conversation concerning the application of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18202 to certain activities engaged in by Drs. Keilin and Gundlach. I do want to reiterate, however, that with respect to proceedings under California Water Code Section 13260 my advice was premised on the understanding that the discharge requirements relate to a single company or entity and involve an attempt by the company or entity to obtain a permit to discharge at a certain level. If, on the other hand, a proceeding involves an attempt to influence a regional quality control board or the state board with respect to discharge requirements that would have general applicability, time spent influencing this type of decision would fall within the time requirements specified in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18239.

I assume this will conclude this matter. If, however, you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Sincerely,

Kenneth Finney
Chief
Legal Division

KF:jo
cc: John Greenwood
